name: ai-clinical-disclaimer
title: AI Clinical Disclaimer
description: Contextual safety notice for generated clinical material, with a provenance badge and caller-authored review boundary.

props.name: severity
props.description: Use `warning` for a real review boundary (unvalidated dosing, out-of-scope data), not for emphasis.

props.name: label
props.description: Provenance badge. Pass null where provenance is already unmistakable.

clinicalNotes: The copy is caller-authored because only you know the boundary that actually applies: what the model saw, what it did not see, and what a clinician must therefore verify. "Summarised from the 12 Mar note only — check the medication list against the chart" changes behaviour; "AI can make mistakes" on every message is wallpaper, and wallpaper is what people stop reading. Reserve `warning` for boundaries with clinical consequence, and never use this component to make an unvalidated output feel sanctioned — if the content should not be acted on at all, do not show it.
notes: Renders an Alert with role="status": contextual, not interruptive. Escalations that must interrupt belong in ai-escalation-alert.
